6a4a89f550c311502adc47e6.question: In this opera, a chorus announces the arrival of a character with the words “Vivat! (“vee-vah”) Vivat! Vivat!” during a torch-lit procession. Carrie Cracknell’s 2024 Met Opera production of this opera sets it in an industrial town by the U.S.-Mexico border. An aria from this opera opens with the arpeggiated cello ostinato [read slowly] “dotted eighth note D, sixteenth note A, eighth notes F, down to A” and was adapted into a (*) fantasy by Pablo de Sarasate. The duet “C’est toi? C’est moi!” (“say TWAH say MWAH”) ends this opera, which features an aria that opens “Votre toast.” In this opera, Escamillo sings the Toreador Song and falls in love with a woman who compares love to a rebellious bird. For 10 points, a Habanera is sung by the title Romani woman of what opera by Georges Bizet?
6a4a89f550c311502adc47e6.question_sanitized: In this opera, a chorus announces the arrival of a character with the words "Vivat! ("vee-vah") Vivat! Vivat!" during a torch-lit procession. Carrie Cracknell's 2024 Met Opera production of this opera sets it in an industrial town by the U.S.-Mexico border. An aria from this opera opens with the arpeggiated cello ostinato [read slowly] "dotted eighth note D, sixteenth note A, eighth notes F, down to A" and was adapted into a (*) fantasy by Pablo de Sarasate. The duet "C'est toi? C'est moi!" ("say TWAH say MWAH") ends this opera, which features an aria that opens "Votre toast." In this opera, Escamillo sings the Toreador Song and falls in love with a woman who compares love to a rebellious bird. For 10 points, a Habanera is sung by the title Romani woman of what opera by Georges Bizet?

6a4a89f750c311502adc48ff.leadin: An opera by this composer features an “Ice Cream Sextet” in which Lippo Fiorentino sings “First time I come to da America / First thing I see is da ice cream.” For 10 points each:
6a4a89f750c311502adc48ff.leadin_sanitized: An opera by this composer features an "Ice Cream Sextet" in which Lippo Fiorentino sings "First time I come to da America / First thing I see is da ice cream." For 10 points each:
parts: Name this composer who collaborated with Elmer Rice and Langston Hughes on the opera Street Scene. This composer’s wife Lotte Lenya had a breakthrough role as the sex worker Jenny in another work that he scored.
Street Scene’s number “Somehow I Never Could Believe” has drawn stylistic comparisons to this Italian verismo composer of Madama Butterfly and Turandot.
Weill also composed the music for this play, whose second act concludes with the song “What Keeps Mankind Alive?” A Moritat singer opens this play by comparing the knife-wielding Macheath to a shark.
parts_sanitized: Name this composer who collaborated with Elmer Rice and Langston Hughes on the opera Street Scene. This composer's wife Lotte Lenya had a breakthrough role as the sex worker Jenny in another work that he scored.
Street Scene's number "Somehow I Never Could Believe" has drawn stylistic comparisons to this Italian verismo composer of Madama Butterfly and Turandot.
Weill also composed the music for this play, whose second act concludes with the song "What Keeps Mankind Alive?" A Moritat singer opens this play by comparing the knife-wielding Macheath to a shark.
answers: Giacomo Puccini [or Giacomo Antonio Domenico Michele Secondo Maria Puccini]
The Threepenny Opera [or Die Dreigroschenoper] (The second line refers to “Mack the Knife.”)
answers_sanitized: Giacomo Puccini [or Giacomo Antonio Domenico Michele Secondo Maria Puccini]
The Threepenny Opera [or Die Dreigroschenoper] (The second line refers to "Mack the Knife.")
